response:
Sec. 1  
In 2019, the Chief Minister of Andhra Pradesh was Y. S. Jaganmohan Reddy. He was the leader of the YSRCP party. He was elected in 2014 and re-elected in 2019.  

***  
Y. S. Jaganmohan Reddy was born on September 12, 1970. He was the son of late Y. S. Rajasekhara Reddy. He was the former Chief Minister of Andhra Pradesh.  

***  
Y. S. Jaganmohan Reddy served as the Chief Minister of Andhra Pradesh from 2014 to 2019. He was the first Chief Minister of the state after its formation. He was known for his development initiatives and welfare programs.  

***  
Y. S. Jaganmohan Reddy's government focused on infrastructure development. He launched several public welfare schemes. His administration emphasized education and healthcare reforms.  

***  
Y. S. Jaganmohan Reddy was a prominent figure in the Telugu political arena. He was known for his charismatic leadership. His party, YSRCP, gained significant support in the state.  

***  
Y. S. Jaganmohan Reddy's tenure was marked by economic growth. He invested in various sectors like agriculture and industry. His policies aimed to improve the quality of life for citizens.  

***  
Y. S. Jaganmohan Reddy faced challenges during his time in office. He dealt with issues like unemployment and corruption. His government worked towards addressing these problems.  

***  
Y. S. Jaganmohan Reddy was re-elected in the 2019 elections. He secured a majority in the state assembly. His leadership continued to shape the political landscape of Andhra Pradesh.
